# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def process_image
    original_file = params[:image]
    desired_format = params[:format] || 'jpg'
    desired_size = params[:size] || '300x300'

    if original_file.present?
      temp_file_path = File.join(Dir.tmpdir, original_file.original_filename)
      File.open(temp_file_path, 'wb') do |file|
        file.write(original_file.read)
      end

      output_file_path = File.join(Rails.root, "public", "processed_#{original_file.original_filename}")
      convert_command = `convert "#{temp_file_path}" -resize #{desired_size} -format #{desired_format} "#{output_file_path}"`
      
      if $?.success?
        render json: { success: true, file_url: "/processed_#{original_file.original_filename}" }
      else
        render json: { success: false, message: 'Image processing failed' }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end

    else
      render json: { success: false, message: 'No image provided' }, status: :bad_request
    end
  rescue => e
    render json: { success: false, message: "An error occurred: #{e.message}" }, status: :internal_server_error
  ensure
    File.delete(temp_file_path) if temp_file_path.present? && File.exist?(temp_file_path)
  end
end
